params.prompt:
Create an ultra-realistic old heritage school classroom interior in the exact same moody aesthetic as the reference image. Keep the same overall composition, same camera placement, and same framing: a wide centered shot from the back half of the classroom looking toward the front teacher area, with strong symmetry and deep room perspective. The camera should be placed at seated eye level / slightly above desk height, facing straight toward the blackboard and teacher desk.

CLASSROOM ARCHITECTURE:
A tall narrow classroom with a very high vaulted ceiling, exposed dark wooden roof trusses, large interior arches, and old colonial / missionary school architecture. The ceiling should have a few hanging pendant lights and ceiling fans, but the practical lights should not dominate. The room should feel old, quiet, slightly dramatic, and realistic.

LAYOUT:
On the RIGHT side of the classroom, place a row of tall arched wooden windows exactly in the spirit of the reference, extending along the wall. Some window panels can be slightly open. Cool blue ambient light should enter from these windows. Outside the windows, dense tropical greenery / palm leaves should be visible, just like an old school building surrounded by lush plants.

On the LEFT side of the classroom, make it a complete solid wall — no windows at all. This wall should have subtle school elements such as framed portraits, notice boards, educational charts, old framed certificates, or wall-mounted school display material. On this LEFT wall, place only ONE entrance door, and that door must be at the starting/front side of the wall from the camera angle — meaning near the teacher’s side/front of the classroom, on the left side of the blackboard wall zone. No other doors on the left wall.

FRONT TEACHER AREA:
At the front center, place a large wall-mounted blackboard. The blackboard must be fixed on the wall, not on a stand. In front of it, place a simple wooden teacher podium / teacher desk centered. Around the front wall, include old cupboards, a small bookshelf, maps, a few framed portraits, and academic wall charts in a restrained and believable way.

FURNITURE:
Fill the room with rows of old wooden student desks and benches, arranged neatly in perspective toward the front. The furniture should be dark polished wood, slightly aged, realistic, and grounded in an old-school aesthetic.

LIGHTING — VERY IMPORTANT:
Match the exact lighting mood of the reference image. The room should be dim and atmospheric, lit mainly by cool blue natural ambient light coming through the right-side windows. The lighting should feel like early dawn / blue-hour morning or rainy low-light ambience. The room must stay dark overall, with soft cool illumination wrapping through the windows and faintly shaping the desks, walls, and teacher area. Do not make it bright. Do not make it sunny. Do not make it warm golden hour. The mood should stay cool, shadowy, moody, cinematic, and calm, with subtle visibility across the room. Keep the light realistic and soft but the image itself must remain crisp and detailed.

STYLE:
Ultra-realistic, 4K, sharp details, realistic wood texture, realistic wall texture, realistic shadows, cinematic old-school atmosphere, grounded, natural, no softness, no blur, no painterly look.

NEGATIVE PROMPT:
windows on left wall, extra doors, more than one entrance door, bright daylight, warm sunlight, golden hour, overexposed room, modern classroom, plastic furniture, whiteboard, blackboard on stand, symmetrical windows on both sides, empty plain left wall with no school material, low detail, blur, soft image, fantasy look, CGI look, distorted perspective, wrong camera angle, off-center composition

Nano Banana Prompt — Opposite Axis Cathedral Interior, Looking Toward Entrance
Use the provided image as the main architectural, lighting, camera, and aesthetic reference. Recreate the same Kolkata cathedral interior, but generate the opposite axis: the camera is now placed near the altar / idol area, looking back toward the main entrance of the church.
Create a wide cinematic interior shot from near the altar, facing the long central aisle toward the cathedral entrance doors. The perspective should feel like the reverse angle of the reference image, as if the viewer has walked to the front altar area and turned around to look back. Preserve the same grand Gothic cathedral architecture: tall pointed arches, ornate painted wall details, stained-glass windows, old cream-and-gold plaster, carved columns, wooden pews on both sides, balcony/gallery structures along the side walls, ceiling fans under the side galleries, decorative religious murals, aged cathedral texture, and the same sacred old Kolkata church atmosphere.
The central aisle should run from the foreground near the altar area toward the distant entrance. Wooden pews should be visible on both sides, aligned symmetrically, with strong depth perspective. The main entrance should be visible far in the background, framed by the tall cathedral arches and shadowed doorway. The door area should feel darker than the altar side, with soft exterior daylight barely glowing around the entrance edges.
Lighting must match the reference exactly: warm golden sunlight streaming diagonally through the side stained-glass windows, visible dust and haze in the air, soft volumetric light rays, gentle bloom, warm highlights on wooden pews, deep but open shadows, no harsh contrast. Keep the same morning cathedral glow, amber-gold shafts of light, sacred atmosphere, and subtle stained-glass color reflections. The light should feel natural and motivated from the windows, not artificial.
Camera should be placed at approximately human eye level or slightly elevated near the altar/idol area, centered on the aisle, using a wide cinematic lens. Maintain the same wide-angle cathedral scale, deep perspective, symmetrical framing, and slight anamorphic softness. The composition should feel grand but real, not fantasy. The foreground may include a subtle hint of altar-side architecture at the edges, but the main focus is the long aisle leading toward the entrance.
Style: ultra-realistic cinematic photography, Kolkata cathedral interior, old Gothic architecture, warm sunrise light, natural haze, volumetric rays, detailed stained glass, aged plaster texture, carved columns, wooden pews, sacred quiet atmosphere, Hollywood film-grade realism, soft lens bloom, natural depth, high detail, no CGI look.
Important continuity rules:
Do not redesign the cathedral. Do not change the architectural language. Do not add modern objects. Do not add people. Do not add extra decorations that are not consistent with the reference. Do not make the space look like a European cathedral; keep the same Kolkata heritage church feel. Keep the lighting, wall colors, stained-glass glow, pew layout, arches, side balconies, and cinematic atmosphere consistent with the reference image.
Negative prompt:
No people, no priests, no tourists, no modern lights, no LED strips, no neon, no fantasy glow, no overexposed white light, no clean new cathedral look, no marble palace look, no extra statues blocking the aisle, no distorted arches, no warped pews, no mismatched architecture, no fisheye distortion, no cartoon, no CGI, no plastic texture, no HDR, no flat lighting, no dark horror mood, no blue night lighting.

soft neon tube edges

soft humid haze

no harsh CGI glow
no hyper-clean digital look

Camera at eye level or slightly low, not a drone shot. The foreground terrace should take up the lower half of the frame with clean negative space. The neon angel sign should sit in the background depth, large enough to influence the frame, but clearly on another building beyond the terrace.

A highly cinematic, photorealistic live-action still of an empty premium interview stage inside a grand contemporary theater, designed for an intimate long-form conversation. Two identical contemporary lounge chairs upholstered in rich charcoal-blue velvet sit facing one another at a subtle conversational angle, perfectly centered on a wide matte theater stage. The chairs feature broad sculpted armrests, gently curved backs, deep seat cushions with natural compression, tapered solid walnut legs, and understated mid-century modern proportions. They are positioned with generous negative space between them, making the empty space itself the emotional focal point of the composition.
The stage is expansive, quiet, and architecturally restrained. A floor-to-ceiling backdrop of deep charcoal-black theatrical velour curtains spans the entire width of the frame, with elegant vertical folds creating subtle rhythm and texture. The curtain is illuminated just enough to reveal its rich woven fabric and layered folds while remaining dark and dramatic. The surrounding proscenium architecture fades naturally into shadow, revealing only faint structural details at the extreme edges of the frame. The theater feels monumental but understated, with no visible audience, no stage equipment, and no visual distractions.

The bulbs provide subtle warmth without blooming excessively or creating harsh flares. Their reflections remain restrained and physically accurate.
The stage floor is a matte charcoal-painted performance floor with realistic surface texture, faint scuff marks from years of use, soft tonal variations, and minimal diffuse reflections. The curtain receives an even, controlled wash that reveals the fabric texture without flattening the scene. The practical bulbs contribute warm ambient highlights while maintaining balanced exposure throughout the frame. The lighting should feel cinematic, premium, and invisible, with no obvious hotspots or dramatic theatrical beams.

SCENE:
"""@img1 — Naisha identity anchor. Maintain exactly.
@img2 — old Kolkata heritage lane during Durga Puja Dashmi evening location reference. Maintain exactly.

Extremely cinematic key still frame.

Old Kolkata heritage lane during Durga Puja Dashmi evening. POV catching @img1 Naisha for the first time through the moving crowd.

Naisha is far in the mid-distance, only partially visible through drifting dhunuchi smoke and between passing devotees. She is laughing softly while moving with the procession. Her deep red saree with gold border and matching sleeveless blouse catch the available light softly within the crowd. Her long side-parted wavy dark hair is open over one shoulder. The feeling should be of an emotionally suspended first glimpse — accidental, fleeting, and unforgettable.

The foreground is busy and layered: shoulders, raised hands, passing bodies, dhak players, and dancers partially interrupting the view. These foreground obstructions should create strong depth and the sensation of searching for her through motion and festival chaos. Naisha should not feel cleanly framed or isolated — she should feel discovered inside the moving crowd.

The deep background holds glowing Durga idol lights, warm yellow practical bulbs, drifting smoke, and the old Kolkata festive lane atmosphere. Wet stone reflections on the ground catch the festival light subtly. The frame should carry a strong foreground-midground-background separation, with Naisha emerging through haze and crowd layers rather than being presented directly.

Lighting should balance warm yellow practical bulbs with cool blue evening ambience, with a soft pink-red bounce coming from sarees and festival lighting. Keep soft shadows, open blacks, no crushed blacks, and a contrast-rich blue-pink-yellow palette. Add gentle halation around practicals, soft lens bloom, atmospheric haze, and natural wet-surface reflections.

Ultra-realistic movie-camera realism. Anamorphic lens. High depth of field. Strong layered depth. Cinematically lit. Emotionally suspended moment. Stunning looking. Old Kolkata Durga Puja devotional chaos, but observed with romantic cinematic sensitivity."""

A highly cinematic, photorealistic live-action establishing location of Kolkata's Maidan on a calm winter morning at approximately 7:45 AM, viewed from a gently elevated vantage point overlooking one of the vast informal football grounds. The composition is extremely wide and airy, emphasizing the immense openness of the Maidan. In the immediate foreground, dew-covered grass is interrupted by naturally worn dirt footpaths that curve gracefully through the field, forming organic leading lines without appearing designed. Dry leaves are scattered across the shaded foreground, while subtle undulations in the terrain reveal years of informal public use.
Slightly left of center in the midground stands a weathered metal football goalpost with no net, leaning almost imperceptibly from age and constant use. Additional smaller goalposts are scattered far across the field, barely visible through the morning haze, suggesting multiple neighborhood football pitches spread across the Maidan. The playing surface is imperfect and authentic—patches of flattened grass, exposed earth around goal mouths, faint bicycle tracks, worn shortcuts and irregular textures accumulated over decades rather than maintained like a stadium.
A continuous line of magnificent mature rain trees stretches across the horizon, their enormous umbrella canopies creating a dark green silhouette softened by delicate winter mist. Rising naturally above this tree line, perfectly centered but partially veiled by atmospheric haze, the white marble dome and towers of the Victoria Memorial emerge with quiet monumentality, integrated into the landscape rather than dominating it. The monument should feel like a distant landmark discovered through the trees instead of a tourist postcard.
The morning sun sits low outside the left edge of the frame, bathing the entire landscape in soft lateral golden light. Thin atmospheric haze diffuses the sunlight into gentle volumetric glow, producing long elegant shadows across the grass while preserving open shadow detail and avoiding harsh contrast. The sky transitions smoothly from pale warm gold near the horizon into clean, desaturated blue overhead, with no dramatic clouds. The atmosphere feels crisp, quiet and freshly awakened, carrying the stillness of early morning before the city fully comes alive.
Material realism is paramount: dew clinging to grass blades, compacted dirt paths with soft edges, scattered fallen leaves beneath the trees, subtle tyre impressions, weathered galvanized steel goalposts with chipped paint and light surface rust, distant bark textures softened by haze and naturally varied grass tones. The environment should feel lived-in through years of informal public use rather than curated or landscaped.
The frame is intentionally empty of principal characters, allowing generous negative space across the field for future blocking. No active football match is taking place; the location itself is the subject. The eye should naturally travel from the foreground paths to the solitary goalpost, across the open field and finally toward the softly glowing Victoria Memorial framed by the tree canopy.
Captured on an ARRI Alexa Mini LF with a 28mm anamorphic prime, positioned approximately 2.5–3 meters above ground level to create a subtle overlook without becoming aerial. Deep focus throughout the landscape, natural film grain, restrained anamorphic bloom, balanced exposure, soft highlight roll-off and realistic dynamic range with open shadows.
Mood: Quiet, expansive, youthful, hopeful and unmistakably Kolkata. A city breathing before the day begins.
Negative prompts: No people, no football players, no vehicles, no stadium seating, no fences surrounding the pitch, no advertising boards, no readable text, no vendors, no litter, no decorative landscaping, no exaggerated fog, no oversaturated greens, no HDR look, no fantasy atmosphere, no CGI textures, no postcard composition, no artificial symmetry, no modern skyline dominating the background.

Create a clean photorealistic character sheet of a heritage Kolkata river boat, inspired by the classic boats seen moored along the Hooghly River during sunrise and sunset. The boat should have a traditional wooden structure, long narrow hull, modest size, slightly raised bow and stern, and a distinct arched canopy in the middle covered with worn fabric or painted panels.
The boat should look old but still operational, with a strong lived-in character: faded multicolor painted surfaces, worn black trim, sun-faded red canopy, scratches, scuffs, patched wood, damp stains, and realistic material variation. It should feel like a real boat from Kolkata’s ghats, not a stylized fantasy design.
Character sheet layout: show full side profile, opposite side profile, front view, rear view, 3/4 hero angle, top-angled view, and 2 close-up detail views showing the wood texture, canopy, seating area, and bow detailing. The boat must remain fully consistent across all views.
Background: plain neutral studio background, clean object-sheet layout, soft diffused lighting, high realism.
Negative prompt: no river scene, no bridge, no people, no modern ferry, no speedboat engine, no glossy showroom finish, no text, no branding, no extra props.

Create a photorealistic asset character sheet of a small traditional Kolkata row boat with a covered center section. The boat should look like a wooden manually operated river boat, built for calm inland and riverbank use. It should have a slim elongated body, flat internal wooden floorboards, a low curved red-brown canopy, simple painted details, and a very local handcrafted design.
The boat should have visible age and long use, including scratches, paint fading, rough wood grain, water staining, rope wear, and slight canopy sag. The design should feel quietly beautiful, functional, and rooted in Kolkata’s riverside life.
Character sheet layout: include front elevation, rear elevation, left side view, right side view, 3/4 front angle, 3/4 rear angle, and detail close-ups of the canopy, side planks, floorboards, and bow shape. Keep proportions and wear patterns completely consistent.
Background: plain neutral character-sheet background, even soft lighting, realistic shadows, no scenery.
Negative prompt: no paddler, no passengers, no luxury wood polish, no fiberglass finish, no modern boating accessories, no text, no labels, no watermark.

Create a cinematic photorealistic character sheet of an old Hooghly River passenger boat from Kolkata. The boat should have a traditional narrow wooden hull, painted in faded blue, black, and red tones, with a semi-cylindrical canopy roof, visible bamboo or wooden support ribs, and a practical open-deck layout.
The boat should feel like it has been in active use for many years, with realistic age and wear: rubbed paint, uneven wood grain, rusty fasteners, weathered canopy, slight dents, muddy waterline marks, and softened edges. It should look like a boat used near Babu Ghat / Prinsep Ghat / the Howrah-side riverbank, deeply rooted in Kolkata’s river culture.
